@graph.name: Business Infinity
@graph.description: Autonomous Strategic Governance platform for founders and enterprise leaders — powered by a legendary AI C-suite.

@graph.name: Business Infinity — Strategy–Execution Divorce
@graph.description: Intent–action gap — the board agrees on strategy, but engineering and sales execute something completely different.

offers.name: The Prime Visionary Agent — Chief Executive Officer
offers.description: The Prime Visionary agent (Chief Executive Officer) addresses strategy–execution divorce through the strategy_execution workflow.

@graph.name: The Entropy Index — Strategy–Execution Divorce
@graph.description: Intent–action gap — the board agrees on strategy, but engineering and sales execute something completely different.

mainEntity.name: Why is my team not executing on the board-approved strategy?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Prime Visionary agent would say: your strategy lives in a slide deck and your execution lives in Jira. The gap between the two is the 'integrity gap' — and it widens with every hire who has never read the original vision.

mainEntity.name: Bridging the gap between OKRs and daily tasks

acceptedAnswer.text: OKRs without enforcement are aspirations, not strategy. A purpose-driven boardroom bridges the gap by scoring every daily task against strategic intent — in real time, not at the quarterly review.

Alignment at 200 people cannot be achieved through meetings — it must be achieved through systems. A boardroom that monitors every project spec and flags 'drift' the moment it appears eliminates the need for alignment meetings entirely.

Cross-functional truth requires a mind that reads across all functions simultaneously. No single dashboard achieves this. A boardroom that connects ERP, CRM, and GitHub sees the contradictions your teams cannot.

mainEntity.name: How to stop sales team from over-promising custom features we can't build

acceptedAnswer.text: Sales over-promising is a symptom of a disconnected product line. The Prime Visionary agent acts as the Product Guardian, bridging CRM and engineering to block orders that violate the semantic purity of the product.

mainEntity.name: Impact of information silos on Series C valuation

acceptedAnswer.text: Information silos are not just inefficient — they are a valuation risk. Due diligence teams see siloed operations as a sign of fragile governance. The fix is not 'integration' — it is unified consciousness.

mainEntity.name: How to stop internal silos from creating conflicting product roadmaps

acceptedAnswer.text: Conflicting roadmaps exist because no single mind holds the complete picture. A boardroom that reads across all project repositories and CRM commitments detects conflicts the moment they are created — not months later.

mainEntity.name: Why is our burn rate increasing faster than our revenue

acceptedAnswer.text: When burn outpaces revenue, the problem is rarely the market — it is internal entropy. Departments optimizing for their own metrics create compound waste. A purpose-driven boardroom enforces whole-company optimization.

mainEntity.name: How to detect 'Quiet Quitting' in middle management

acceptedAnswer.text: Quiet quitting is invisible to standard HR metrics. It shows up as decreased initiative, risk-avoidance, and 'safe' decisions. The People Strategist agent analyzes communication patterns and decision quality to detect disengagement before it spreads.

Alignment is not a survey score — it is the ratio of actions that serve the stated purpose to actions that serve local interests. A boardroom that reads across all systems measures this ratio in real time.

description: Intent–action gap — the board agrees on strategy, but engineering and sales execute something completely different.
symptom: Shadow projects discovered months in. Sales promises features engineering deprecated. Resources wasted on conflicting roadmaps.
